file.content:
services: 
  wordpress:
    container_name: honehealth_lp_wp
    image: wordpress:6.8.1-php8.2
    ports:
      - "8081:80"
    environment:
      WORDPRESS_DB_HOST: db
      WORDPRESS_DB_USER: awesumsawz
      WORDPRESS_DB_PASSWORD: dukesilver
      WORDPRESS_DB_NAME: honehealth_lp_db
    volumes:
      - ./:/var/tmp:delegated
      - ./buy-honehealth-com/themes/HoneLandingPages:/var/www/html/wp-content/themes/HoneLandingPages:delegated
      - ./plugins/:/var/www/html/wp-content/plugins:delegated
      - ./mu-plugins/:/var/www/html/wp-content/mu-plugins:delegated
      - ./uploads/:/var/www/html/wp-content/uploads:delegated
      - ./debug.log:/var/www/html/wp-content/debug.log:delegated
      - ./wp-config.php:/var/www/html/wp-config.php:delegated
      - ./custom-php.ini:/usr/local/etc/php/conf.d/custom-php.ini:delegated
  
  db:
    container_name: honehealth_lp_db
    image: mysql:8.0
    ports:
      - "34307:3306"
    environment: 
      MYSQL_DATABASE: honehealth_lp_db
      MYSQL_USER: awesumsawz
      MYSQL_PASSWORD: dukesilver
      MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: awesome1234
    volumes:
      - ./mysql/my.cnf:/etc/mysql/conf.d/my.cnf:delegated
      - ./mysql/lib:/var/lib/mysql:delegated

  backstop:
    container_name: honehealth_lp_backstop
    image: backstopjs/backstopjs:6.3.23
    entrypoint: ["/bin/sh", "/src/run.sh"]
    volumes:
      - ./backstop:/src
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    profiles:
      - testing

content.text:
`longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages` already exists as a real directory with content. To make it a symlink pointing to `main/themes/HoneLandingPages`, I'd need to remove it first.

Is it safe to delete `longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages/`? It looks like a copy of the theme (has `build/`, `build.js`, etc). The real source will be `main/themes/HoneLandingPages/`.

questions.question: longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages/ already exists as a real directory with content (build/, src/, etc). To create the symlink, I need to remove it. Is that safe?

options.label: Yes, remove it
options.description: Delete it and replace with a symlink → main/themes/HoneLandingPages/

options.label: No, keep it
options.description: I'll abort — tell me the correct approach

content.content: User has answered your questions: "longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages/ already exists as a real directory with content (build/, src/, etc). To create the symlink, I need to remove it. Is that safe?"="No, keep it". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.

options.label: Yes, remove it

options.label: No, keep it

content.text: Got it. Let me clarify the intended symlink direction then.

questions.question: Which direction should the symlink go?

options.label: main/ → longevity-lp
options.description: Create main/themes/HoneLandingPages as a symlink pointing to longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages/ (longevity-lp is the source of truth)

options.label: longevity-lp/ → main
options.description: Replace longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages with a symlink to main/themes/HoneLandingPages/ (main is the source of truth) — but this requires removing the existing dir
